checks.a_analysis: The content from page 21 of the source PDF, specifically the list under 'SIP Admin Service Facts and Benefits', appears twice in the output. It is included at the end of the first section ('sections[0].elements[14]') and then repeated in the second section ('sections[1].elements[1]'). This content only appears once in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 21: 'SIP Admin Service Facts and Benefits' followed by a bulleted list starting with 'Admin calls will terminate on the hosted call handling equipment (Raleigh/Columbia)'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list is duplicated at 'sections[0].elements[14]' and 'sections[1].elements[1]'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 1 and 2, element indices 14 and 1 respectively, corresponding to source page 21.

checks.a_analysis: The document's heading hierarchy contains a skip in the second section. The section title 'SIP Admin Service for Hosted PSAPs' acts as a level 1 heading, but it is immediately followed by a level 3 heading 'SIP Admin' ('sections[1].elements[1]') without an intervening level 2 heading. This breaks the sequential nesting required for accessibility.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 21: 'SIP Admin Service for Hosted PSAPs' (Title) and 'SIP Admin' (Diagram label).
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 2, element index 1, corresponding to page 21.

checks.a_analysis: The table on page 16 ('Pilot PSAPs') has been correctly converted. The output preserves the two-column structure (PSAP Name, Date Joined the Pilot), correctly identifies headers, and includes all data rows faithfully to the source.

The output fails to preserve the reading order of the source document. The first 10 pages of the PDF, which include the meeting agenda, minutes, and incident review, are entirely missing from the start of the document. Instead, a placeholder section titled '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' is placed at the very end of the JSON ('sections[2]'). This fundamentally alters the document's logical structure and flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 1-10 contain the Meeting Agenda, Minutes, and Post Incident Review.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON starts with content from Page 11 and places a placeholder for pages 1-11 in 'sections[2]'.

checks.a_analysis: Lists throughout the document, including the nested list on page 14 regarding DIT tasks, are accurately converted with correct nesting and bullet styles.
No broken, fabricated, or missing links were found within the converted text portions. While links on page 4 are missing, this is due to the omission of those pages entirely (audited under S4).

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output is missing more than half of the source document. Pages 1 through 11 of the source PDF, which include the meeting agenda, attendance records, meeting minutes, and an incident review, are not rendered in the HTML. A placeholder note at the bottom of the HTML output confirms this failure, stating that content from pages 1-11 could not be converted. This represents a catastrophic failure of visual organization and document flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 21 pages of structured content including an Agenda (p.1), Meeting Minutes (p.6-9), and Post Incident Review (p.10).
checks.b_evidence_output: "[Pages 1-11 unavailable] Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted. This section may be available after retry."
checks.b_evidence_location: The entire first half of the document (Pages 1-11) is missing.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document fails to render several tables present in the source PDF. Specifically, the 'Attendance' table on page 6 and the 'Minutes Vote' table on page 7 are entirely absent due to the conversion failure of the first 11 pages. While the 'Pilot PSAPs' table on page 16 is correctly rendered, the complete loss of other data tables is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'Attendance' table on page 6 and the 'Minutes Vote' table on page 7.
checks.b_evidence_output: These tables are completely missing from the HTML output.

checks.a_analysis: Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML rendering. This includes agency logos on the first page, headers on subsequent pages, and a critical data chart titled 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' on page 3. While some diagrams from later pages (e.g., page 14, 18, 21) are present, the absence of the page 3 chart and identifying logos is a significant visual loss.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'NC DIT' logo (Page 1) and the 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' bar chart (Page 3).
checks.b_evidence_output: These images are absent from the HTML rendering.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output contains a visual duplication of a major content block. The section titled 'SIP Admin Service for Hosted PSAPs' along with its bulleted list 'SIP Admin Service Facts and Benefits' (derived from page 21 of the PDF) is rendered twice in immediate succession at the end of the HTML document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The section 'SIP Admin Service for Hosted PSAPs' and its associated list 'SIP Admin Service Facts and Benefits' on page 21.
checks.b_evidence_output: The heading 'SIP Admin Service for Hosted PSAPs' and the following 12-item list are repeated twice in the HTML output.
checks.b_evidence_location: At the end of the HTML document, before the 'SIP Admin' architecture diagram section.
